title: "I Asked No Other Thing,"
content:
I asked no other thing,
    No other was denied.
    I offered Being for it;
    The mighty merchant smiled.

    Brazil? He twirled a button,
    Without a glance my way:
    "But, madam, is there nothing else
    That we can show to-day?"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled ""I Asked No Other Thing,"", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
